title: Potable Gold: 250 years of Madeira in America
summary: The wine that witnessed the Declaration of Independence...
content: <div class="author-description"><p>As a graduate of the University of Virginia, Lauren Mowery first developed a taste for wine as a student in winery-rich Charlottesville. Graduating Fordham Law, she took a career detour as a New York litigator before leaving to pursue wine and travel writing full time, for which she has won several awards. Mowery was travel editor for Wine Enthusiast for four years and a Forbes wine and travel columnist for six years, in addition to contributing to dozens of other drinks publications including Tasting Panel, Somm Journal, Punch and SevenFifty Daily. She hopes to finish her Master of Wine by 2024. When not on the road, she splits her time between upstate New York and Charleston, South Carolina.</p></div>

title: Rebel of Provence: Clos Cibonne and the cult of Tibouren
summary: The family that stuck to its guns...
content: <div class="author-description"><p>Elizabeth Gabay MW has specialised in the wines of south-eastern France and Hungary since the 1980’s. Working as an independent wine merchant and consultant, she graduated as a Master of Wine in 1998 and moved to southeast France in 2002.</p><p>Her book, Rose: Understanding the pink wine revolution, was published in 2018 and she has continued to write about and judge rosé wines for Decanter.</p><p>Aside from Decanter, she has written for Drinks Business, Harpers, The Wine Merchant, VinCE and Nomacorc.</p><p>She is the lead instructor for the Provence immersion course run by the French Wine Society and she has judged at numerous Decanter World Wine Awards since 2007.</p></div>

title: The vanishing of Chiroubles – is this Beaujolais cru in danger of disappearing?
summary: Beaujolais' highest cru offers heartbreak in every sip...
content: <div class="author-description"><p>Natasha Hughes MW began her career in the wine trade as deputy editor of <a href="http://www.Decanter.com">Decanter.com</a>. She left the magazine in 2001 and has since enjoyed a thriving freelance career as a writer and consultant. Writing about wine and food, Hughes has contributed to specialist publications across the world, and has acted as a consultant to private clients, wineries and restaurants. In addition, she hosts wine seminars and tastings, and has judged globally at wine competitions. Hughes graduated as a Master of Wine in 2014, winning four out of the seven available prizes at graduation, including the Outstanding Achievement Award.</p></div>

title: Sweet memories: The unlikely winemaking career of Miguel Martínez and Rioja's forgotten sweet wines
summary: Historic Rioja reborn...
content: <div class="author-description"><p>Noah Chichester is a wine writer, educator and founder of<strong><a href="https://winesofgalicia.com/"> winesofgalicia.com</a></strong> - the only English-language website dedicated to the study of Galician wine. He created The Wines of Galicia after spending four years living in Spain,  immersed in Galician wine and culture. In addition to The Wines of Galicia, he has written for SevenFifty Daily, GuildSomm, and Fodor's.</p></div>

title: Panel tasting results: North Italian Pinot Grigio bursting with character
summary: Insipid? Tasteless? Think again...
content: <p>Melania Battiston is wine director at Michelin one-star Cornus in London. Hailing from Lombardy, Italy, she’s a CMS Advanced Sommelier and runs Mel CnC, her consultancy for hospitality and brands</p>

title: New Zealand's aromatic whites: Tailor-made for summer
summary: Ethereal perfumes from The Long White Cloud...
content: <div class="author-description"><p>Following a Diploma of Viticulture and Winemaking, Emma Jenkins achieved the Master of Wine qualification in 2011, becoming the ninth New Zealander to do so. She is a wine consultant and also writes for several wine publications, including <i>The Independent Wine Monthly </i>which she co-edits with Jane Skilton MW. A former judge at the Decanter World Wine Awards, Jenkins also judges at other local and international competitions. She teaches Wine and Spirit Education Trust courses and is the Master of Wine Research Paper Chair.</p></div>

title: World Cup of Wines: We pick two final-worthy wines from Spain and Argentina
summary: Only the very best will do...
content: null

title: Why higher-proof blanco tequila is a hot spirits trend
summary: 'The agave flavours hit you in waves...'
content: <p>Carissa Chesanek is a food and spirits writer based in Brooklyn. Her work has been seen in <em>Imbibe, InsideHook, Food Network, Vinepair, Whisky Advocate</em>, and<em> Alcohol Professor</em>. When she's not writing, she's on the hunt for the best Old Fashioned.</p>

title: Editors’ picks: A range of great wines to try – July 2026
summary: See what our team has been trying recently...
content: <div class="author-description"><p>Content written and compiled by the Decanter Team</p></div>

title: Sam Neill: A tribute and recent interview with the late winemaker and actor
summary: Acclaimed actor and tireless advocate for New Zealand wine...
content: <div class="author-description"><p class="p1"><span class="s1">Julie Sheppard joined the Decanter team in 2018 and is Regional Editor for Australia, New Zealand and South Africa &amp; Spirits Editor. </span></p><p class="p1"><span class="s1">Before Decanter, she worked for a range of drinks and food titles, including as managing editor of both <em>Imbibe</em> and <em>Square Meal</em>, associate publisher of </span><em>The Drinks Business</em><span class="s1">, senior editor of the Octopus Publishing Group and Supplements editor of <em>Harpers Wine &amp; Spirit</em>. As a contributor, she has over 20 years’ experience writing <span class="Apple-converted-space">about food, drink and travel </span>for a wide range of publications, including <em><span class="Apple-converted-space">Condé Nast Traveller, </span></em></span><span class="s1"><em>Delicious</em>, <em>Waitrose Kitchen</em>, <em>Waitrose Drinks</em>, <em>Time Out</em> and national newspapers including <em>The Telegraph</em> and <em>The Sunday Times</em>.</span></p><p class="p1"><span class="s1">.</span></p></div>
